claim: 4 of 12 columns in DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.BASE are Excel formula-derived: COD_RT (XLOOKUP triple-condition over RUTA_RUTERO), NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR (VLOOKUP over COD_RT), OTROS (XLOOKUP quadruple-condition over FOCO_CLIENTE Tabla3), duplicados (COUNTIFS self-referencing flag). The remaining 8 columns are SOURCE_DIRECT (pasted from external retail report).
evidence: research/claude/runs/CLAUDE_DATA_LINEAGE_0003A_INVENTORY/07_findings.md section 2
openpyxl formula string extraction from BASE rows 2-5
DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx BASE sheet: 43635 rows
business_impact: Formula correctness depends on RUTA_RUTERO and FOCO_CLIENTE being current at save time; stale values load silently.
technical_impact: 174,540 formula cells recalculated per workbook open; loader reads cached values, not live formulas.
proposed_action: Document formula dependency chain in loader README as prerequisite validation step.

claim: The duplicados column in BASE (COUNTIFS detecting repeated rows by [FECHA, MARCA, SKU, COD_RT]) is redundant for the loader. scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py performs Python drop_duplicates() independently; the column triggers an extra_columns:duplicados warning in source_check and is otherwise silently ignored. The UPSERT conflict key is (fecha, cod_rt, sku, marca), meaning DB deduplication is also in place.
evidence: scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py source_check function: extra_columns warning branch
research/claude/runs/CLAUDE_DATA_LINEAGE_0003A_INVENTORY/07_findings.md section 3 duplicados formula
UPSERT ON CONFLICT (fecha, cod_rt, sku, marca) in load_fact_from_excel.py
business_impact: None: redundant flag does not affect loaded data.
technical_impact: Loader issues a monitoring warning signal for a column it silently ignores.
proposed_action: Remove duplicados from BASE at next RUTA_RUTERO update cycle; no loader change needed.

claim: COD_RT XLOOKUP evaluates a triple-condition boolean product (CADENA * N_LOCAL * MARCA) over all 3,568 RUTA_RUTERO rows for each of 43,635 BASE rows: up to ~155 million cell comparisons per workbook recalculation. Loader correctness depends entirely on the workbook being saved with current computed values; stale saves ingest prior-period COD_RT without warning.
evidence: research/claude/runs/CLAUDE_DATA_LINEAGE_0003A_INVENTORY/07_findings.md section 3 COD_RT formula
RUTA_RUTERO 3568 rows (openpyxl ws.max_row)
BASE 43635 rows
scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py: no formula-recency validation before read
business_impact: Stale COD_RT silently corrupts CG metrics for affected weeks.
technical_impact: Loader has no defense against stale formula values; openpyxl reads cached cell values only.
proposed_action: Add a loader pre-flight: compare workbook mtime against expected weekly update window and warn if stale.

claim: data/DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx is a manually maintained local archive of historical stock/sales data not part of the automated pipeline. Its schema is identical to BASE (same 12 columns), row count (141,886) is consistent with multiple BASE-load periods (~3.25 loads of 43,635 rows), and it has no references in any script. It appears to have been built by accumulating saved BASE copies.
evidence: Schema identity confirmed via openpyxl profiling of both files
No loader reference: grep across scripts/, app/, tests/ yields zero matches
Last modified 2026-04-08 vs INVENTARIO 2026-06-08 (62-day gap)
141886 / 43635 approx 3.25 historical periods
business_impact: If users query this file for historical analysis, data may diverge from public.fact_stock_venta.
technical_impact: Maintenance burden without automated refresh; risk of branching historical versions.
proposed_action: Codex to check if public.fact_stock_venta history covers the same period range. If yes, flag for retirement.

claim: COD_RT and NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR could be computed in Python at load time using public.ruta_rutero (already in DB), eliminating the Excel formula dependency and stale-value risk. Join keys: CADENA + N_LOCAL (as COD_B2B) + MARCA (as CLIENTE) for COD_RT, then COD_RT for NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR. Blockers: (a) N_LOCAL integer vs COD_B2B type compatibility, (b) RUTA_RUTERO duplicate COD_KPI_ONE handling.
evidence: research/claude/runs/CLAUDE_DATA_LINEAGE_0003A_INVENTORY/07_findings.md sections 3, 6, 8
scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py writes to public.ruta_rutero
scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py already applies str.upper() and to_sku_text normalization
business_impact: Eliminates one class of silent data error for the most business-critical derived column.
technical_impact: Reduces Excel pre-computation steps from 3 to 1 after implementation.
proposed_action: Codex to build parity test: Python-computed COD_RT vs Excel-formula COD_RT on one historical week. Not for production until parity confirmed.

claim: scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py computes row_hash as MD5 of 29 business fields per row, stores it in public.ruta_rutero.row_hash and cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows.row_hash. The loader explicitly comments: 'row_hash (fingerprint negocio: util para analisis; NO es llave de UPSERT)'. The UPSERT conflict key is (source, source_row).
evidence: scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py: row_hash computation block and comment string
scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py: ON CONFLICT (source, source_row) DO UPDATE SET ...
business_impact: row_hash enables route change detection but its consumer is unknown; if unused it adds load cost for zero gain.
technical_impact: MD5 computation per row (29 fields) adds O(n) hashing cost on every ruta load.
proposed_action: Codex to search DB queries and reports for row_hash usage before flagging as removable.

claim: The ruta loader writes to 3 DB destinations: (1) public.ruta_rutero (primary routing master, UPSERT on (source, source_row)); (2) cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows (full load history including payload_json of all row fields as JSON); (3) cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_batch (batch metadata). Every load accumulates a full snapshot of all 3,568 rows in ruta_rutero_load_rows.
evidence: scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py: insert_rows() targeting cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows
scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py: insert_batch() targeting cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_batch
scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py: upsert_ruta_rutero() targeting public.ruta_rutero
business_impact: cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows grows by 3,568 rows per load event; without cleanup it accumulates indefinitely.
technical_impact: payload_json column stores all 30 fields as JSON per row per batch; significant storage for audit-only data.
proposed_action: Codex to check cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows row count and establish a retention policy if no consumer found.

claim: row_hash stored in public.ruta_rutero and cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows is not read by any function in app/db.py, app/screens/*, or app/services/*. No runtime application benefit from the hash computation is confirmed. The ruta_rutero_load_rows history table appears used only for manual DB-level auditing.

technical_impact: O(n) hash computation on each load and accumulating history table with no confirmed runtime consumer.
proposed_action: Codex to verify by DB query whether ruta_rutero_load_rows is used in any stored procedure or report outside app/db.py.

claim: scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py is the largest single script in the codebase at 2,023 lines and 54 functions. It loads 3 source sheets (KPIONE, KPIONE2, POWER_APP) into cg_raw.kpione_raw, cg_raw.kpione2_raw, cg_raw.power_app_raw, and writes batch metadata to cg_audit.batch_registry.
evidence: wc -l scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py: 2023
grep -c 'def ' scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py: 54
scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py: SHEET_SOURCE_MAP and write targets
business_impact: High complexity increases bug surface area and onboarding time.
technical_impact: Single file combines schema validation, normalization, hashing, DB write, and audit logging: high coupling.
proposed_action: Document function inventory as reference for future refactoring planning.

claim: load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py implements a sheet-hash-skip mechanism: SHA256 of the full Excel sheet (column names + all cell values as strings) stored in cg_audit.batch_registry.notes as JSON key sheet_hash. If the hash matches the previous batch for the same source, the loader skips the sheet entirely, preventing redundant Supabase writes when the workbook is re-loaded without upstream data changes.
evidence: scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py: compute_sheet_hash() function using hashlib.sha256
scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py: batch_registry notes JSON with sheet_hash key
scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py: hash comparison skip logic
business_impact: Prevents double-counting in cg_raw tables when operator re-runs loader on unchanged Excel.
technical_impact: Effective anti-bloat guard; reduces unnecessary DB writes significantly for unchanged weeks.
proposed_action: Document in loader README as an operational guarantee.

claim: All 3 cg_raw tables (kpione_raw, kpione2_raw, power_app_raw) include a payload_json column storing the complete source row as a JSON dict alongside individual parsed columns. No function in app/db.py reads from any cg_raw table; the application reads exclusively from cg_core views and cg_mart facts.

business_impact: payload_json provides raw audit capability; value depends on whether it is ever queried for diagnostics.
technical_impact: payload_json approximately doubles per-row write size in cg_raw tables.
proposed_action: Codex to check if any stored procedure, view, or DB function reads payload_json from cg_raw tables.

claim: scripts/refresh_control_gestion_v2_incremental.py implements a 4-stage pipeline: (1) reads from cg_core.v_cg_visita_dia_precedencia_v2 and cg_core.v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2; (2) creates temp table _cg_daily_stage; (3) inserts into cg_mart.fact_cg_visita_dia_resuelta_v2 (daily); (4) aggregates to cg_mart.fact_cg_out_weekly_v2 then refreshes cg_mart.mv_cg_out_weekly_v2. Script is 1,672 lines and 45 functions.
evidence: scripts/refresh_control_gestion_v2_incremental.py: pipeline function names confirmed
grep for cg_core.v_cg_visita_dia_precedencia_v2 in refresh script
grep for cg_mart.fact_cg_out_weekly_v2 in refresh script
wc -l: 1672; grep -c def: 45
business_impact: This script is the critical link between cg_raw sources and the app-visible CG screen data.
technical_impact: Failure in any stage leaves cg_mart in a partially-updated state; no rollback mechanism identified in static review.
proposed_action: Document pipeline stages and failure modes. Codex to verify transaction scope of each write stage.

claim: 10 functions in app/db.py have zero static callers anywhere in app/, scripts/, tests/: get_result_view_contract, get_mercaderistas_home, get_locales_por_mercaderista, get_tabla_ux_paginada, get_scope_clientes_rr, get_rr_stock_en_local, get_cobertura_local, get_clientes_sin_match_rr_stock, get_cg_v2_contract_smoke, get_cg_v2_filter_options. This extends the 5 functions already VALIDATED in AI_SHARED_MEMORY with 5 new candidates.
evidence: grep -r for each function name across app/, scripts/, tests/: only definition found, no callers
VALIDATED functions (get_scope_clientes_rr, get_rr_stock_en_local, get_cobertura_local, get_clientes_sin_match_rr_stock): confirmed re-checked
New 5 confirmed in this pass: get_result_view_contract, get_mercaderistas_home, get_locales_por_mercaderista, get_tabla_ux_paginada, get_cg_v2_contract_smoke, get_cg_v2_filter_options
business_impact: Dead functions increase cognitive load; no functional impact while dead.
technical_impact: ~300-500 lines of SQL-bearing code with associated DB views as dependencies.

claim: app/db.py (5,347 lines) contains 80 SQL f-triple-string blocks, 35 JOIN clauses, 15 CTE (WITH) patterns, and 54 os.getenv() calls. All DB object names are injected as f-string interpolations from environment variables, making them fully configurable but not statically auditable without env context.
evidence: Python/bash heredoc scripts counting f-strings, JOINs, CTEs, os.getenv in app/db.py
54 os.getenv calls confirmed
9 CG_V2_* constants loaded at module level from env (lines ~110-120 of db.py)
business_impact: f-string SQL blocks are safe for internal use; no user input flows into view names.
technical_impact: 80 f-string SQL blocks make static SQL linting impossible without env resolution.
proposed_action: Document the 9 CG_V2_* constants in deployment runbook.

claim: All module-level imports in app/Home.py (db, exports, screen modules) are deferred inside the main() function body (lines 248-261), not at module level. Streamlit does not eager-import these modules during server startup; they load only on the first user session that reaches main().
evidence: app/Home.py lines 248-261: from app import db; from app.exports import (...); from app.screens.* imports
No import of db or screen modules found at module level of Home.py (outside main())
business_impact: No business impact; cold-start behavior is consistent with deferred import pattern.
technical_impact: First user session pays full import cost. Subsequent sessions reuse imported modules per Streamlit module cache.
proposed_action: Document this pattern in AGENTS.md as intentional design.

claim: The 10 zero-caller db.py functions split into two functional groups: (a) Coverage audit (get_scope_clientes_rr, get_rr_stock_en_local, get_cobertura_local, get_clientes_sin_match_rr_stock): incomplete feature for client coverage reporting never wired to a screen. (b) CG v2 introspection (get_cg_v2_contract_smoke, get_cg_v2_filter_options): CG v2 diagnostic utilities never exposed in UI. Remaining 4 appear to be abandoned UI feature stubs.
evidence: Function names and SQL patterns suggest cohesive feature groupings
Coverage audit group matches backlog DB-001
CG v2 introspection matches CG implementation kernel validation patterns
business_impact: Coverage audit feature if completed would provide client-route match quality metrics currently unavailable.
technical_impact: Each group may reference DB views exclusively serving these functions; removing functions may enable view cleanup.
proposed_action: Codex to confirm which DB views are exclusively referenced by the dead functions and have no other consumer.

claim: app/db.py references DB objects across 5 schemas: public (fact_stock_venta, ruta_rutero, ~8 stock/client views/MVs), cg_raw (kpione_raw, kpione2_raw, power_app_raw: write-only from app; no db.py read calls), cg_core (routing views, ruta history: read by refresh script), cg_mart (fact_cg_visita_dia_resuelta_v2, fact_cg_out_weekly_v2, mv_cg_out_weekly_v2: read by app), cg_audit (batch_registry: write-only from app). Approximately 28 distinct DB objects referenced across all code.
evidence: grep for schema-prefixed references in app/db.py: cg_mart., cg_core., cg_raw., cg_audit., public.
9 CG_V2_* env constants in db.py lines 110-120
sql/03_cliente_scope_materialized_views.sql: 3 public schema MVs
business_impact: Full schema map needed before any DB object changes to avoid breaking view dependencies.
technical_impact: 5 schemas with ~28 objects; cg_raw is write-only from app perspective.
proposed_action: Codex to produce authoritative DB object catalog distinguishing app-facing vs pipeline-internal objects.

claim: 9 CG_V2_* constants in app/db.py are loaded from os.getenv() at module import time with schema-prefixed defaults: CG_V2_PRECEDENCIA_VIEW, CG_V2_BASE_SEMANA_VIEW, CG_V2_BASE_MV, CG_V2_OUT_WEEKLY_VIEW, CG_V2_OUT_WEEKLY_MV, CG_V2_FACT_DAILY, CG_V2_FACT_WEEKLY, CG_V2_FRECUENCIA_BASE_VIEW, CG_V2_SCOPE_VIEW. Mismatched env values will silently query non-existent DB objects.
evidence: app/db.py lines ~110-120: CG_V2_* constant definitions with os.getenv() and default values
grep CG_V2_OUT_WEEKLY_VIEW in db.py: referenced at lines 2927 and 5037
business_impact: Env misconfiguration causes CG screen to fail silently or show empty results.
technical_impact: 9 env keys must match DB object names; no startup validation exists to catch mismatches.
proposed_action: Add a startup probe checking each CG_V2_* env-resolved name against pg_catalog before the first user request.

claim: cg_raw tables (kpione_raw, kpione2_raw, power_app_raw) are written by the CG loader but never read by app/db.py. The application accesses CG data exclusively through cg_core views and cg_mart facts. The cg_raw layer is a raw audit/staging layer decoupled from the runtime, accumulating indefinitely without app-level visibility into its size.
evidence: grep for 'kpione_raw', 'kpione2_raw', 'power_app_raw' in app/db.py: zero matches
scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py: all writes target cg_raw.*
scripts/refresh_control_gestion_v2_incremental.py: reads from cg_core.*, not cg_raw.*
business_impact: No direct business impact; risk if Supabase storage tier has row or space limits.
technical_impact: cg_raw growth driven entirely by loader frequency and sheet-hash-skip misses.
proposed_action: Codex to check current cg_raw table row counts and establish a data retention window (e.g., last 12 weeks).

claim: The end-to-end weekly inventory load requires at least 7 manual steps: (1) obtain retail report from external chain; (2) paste rows A-H into BASE; (3) update RUTA_RUTERO if new locals exist; (4) update FOCO_CLIENTE if new focus flags apply; (5) wait for Excel formula recalculation (10-60s for COD_RT XLOOKUP on 43,635 rows); (6) save workbook; (7) run load_fact_from_excel.py. Steps 3-6 can introduce silent data errors if skipped.
evidence: research/claude/runs/CLAUDE_DATA_LINEAGE_0003A_INVENTORY/07_findings.md section 7
DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx formula structure confirmed by openpyxl
scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py: no pre-flight check for formula freshness
business_impact: Each skipped step is a silent data quality failure; most critical is step 6 (stale formula values loaded to DB).
technical_impact: No automated validation exists between step 6 and step 7 to detect formula staleness.
proposed_action: Add a loader pre-flight: compare workbook file mtime against a configurable max-age threshold and warn before loading.

claim: The OTROS column (FOCO_CLIENTE XLOOKUP, quadruple-condition) has a ~2% non-empty hit rate (848 source rows vs 43,635 BASE rows). A Python lookup at load time against a materialized FOCO_CLIENTE table would eliminate the Excel formula dependency, reduce recalculation time, and make the FOCO_CLIENTE data source explicit in the loader.
evidence: FOCO_CLIENTE (Tabla3) has 848 rows (openpyxl max_row)
BASE has 43,635 rows
XLOOKUP formula: FECHA + CADENA + N_LOCAL + SKU quadruple match against Tabla3[ESTATUS]
research/claude/runs/CLAUDE_DATA_LINEAGE_0003A_INVENTORY/07_findings.md section 6 OTROS row
business_impact: Low individual impact; combined with COD_RT offload would eliminate all formula pre-computation requirements.
technical_impact: FOCO_CLIENTE would need to be maintained as a DB table or CSV, not inside Excel.
proposed_action: Evaluate after COD_RT parity test is complete. Lower priority than INVDATA-HYP-001.

claim: The sql/ directory contains 8 SQL files: sql/01-03 are schema creation files (committed); sql/04-08 are CG v2 DDL drafts (committed, _draft suffix); sql/09 (09_control_gestion_kpione2_raw_dryrun_draft.sql) and sql/10 (10_control_gestion_kpione2_forward_recovery_lab_draft.sql) are untracked new files (git status ?? prefix). No evidence these draft files create live objects in the current Supabase instance.
evidence: git status output: ?? sql/09_control_gestion_kpione2_raw_dryrun_draft.sql
git status output: ?? sql/10_control_gestion_kpione2_forward_recovery_lab_draft.sql
All sql/04-10 filenames contain _draft suffix
business_impact: Draft SQL files without clear status create uncertainty about what has been applied to production.
technical_impact: Untracked SQL files risk being applied accidentally or lost; no migration tracking system visible.
proposed_action: Establish convention: committed draft files are proposal-only; applied files move to a migrations/ directory with timestamp prefix.

claim: DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx contains an internal worksheet named DB_HISTORICA (244,008 rows, Power Query-derived from BASE) alongside the external file DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx (141,886 rows, no loader reference). The internal DB_HISTORICA has 71% more rows than the external file. Both have schema identical to BASE (12 columns).
evidence: data/DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx profiled: sheet DB_HISTORICA 244,008 rows
data/DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx profiled: Hoja1 141,886 rows
Both confirmed as 12-column schema matching BASE
business_impact: Two separate historical archives with divergent row counts creates ambiguity about authoritative historical source.
technical_impact: 244,008 rows inside a 21 MB workbook contributes to slow Excel open/recalculation times.
proposed_action: Clarify with Bastian which archive is authoritative and whether DB (fact_stock_venta) can replace both.

claim: Historical stock/sales data is maintained simultaneously in at least 3 layers: (1) DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.DB_HISTORICA internal sheet (244,008 rows, Power Query from BASE); (2) DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx external file (141,886 rows, manual); (3) public.fact_stock_venta (DB-backed, size unknown without query). Divergent row counts and no synchronization mechanism create a compound source-of-truth problem.
evidence: CLAUDE-C001-COMPLEXITY-FACT-002 (internal workbook DB_HISTORICA vs external xlsx)
CLAUDE-C001-INVDATA-INF-001 (DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO as manual archive)
scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py writes to public.fact_stock_venta on each load
business_impact: Historical trend analyses may differ depending on which archive is queried; no single source of truth.
technical_impact: 3-layer history adds maintenance burden; each layer must be checked independently.
proposed_action: Codex to determine date range coverage of public.fact_stock_venta. If it covers all loaded history, both Excel archives become redundant.

claim: If public.fact_stock_venta contains complete load history (same or greater date range as DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx), then DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx could be retired as a manual archive redundant with the DB. This would eliminate one historical data inconsistency source. Requires Codex to confirm DB coverage before any governance decision.

public.fact_stock_venta: UPSERT target of load_fact_from_excel.py since loader inception
CLAUDE-C001-COMPLEXITY-INF-001: 3-layer history problem
business_impact: Retiring Excel archive reduces confusion and maintenance cost; risk if DB history is incomplete.
technical_impact: No code change required; retirement is a data governance decision.
proposed_action: Codex to run: SELECT MIN(fecha), MAX(fecha), COUNT(*) FROM public.fact_stock_venta and compare against DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO date range.

claim: The payload_json columns in cg_raw.kpione_raw, cg_raw.kpione2_raw, and cg_raw.power_app_raw duplicate all source row fields already stored in individual typed columns. If no DB function, view, or stored procedure reads payload_json from cg_raw tables, this column could be eliminated from future loader writes, reducing Supabase write size by an estimated 40-60% per row for the CG load step. The sheet-hash-skip mechanism provides sufficient batch-level audit trail.
evidence: CLAUDE-C001-LOADER-FACT-003 (payload_json in cg_raw; no app/db.py consumer found)
CLAUDE-C001-LOADER-FACT-002 (sheet-hash-skip via batch_registry provides batch-level audit)
JSON overhead: all row fields serialized as JSON string approximately equals individual column byte count
business_impact: Reducing write volume lowers Supabase row storage for a high-frequency write operation.
technical_impact: Loader simplification: remove payload_json construction from insert_rows(). DB migration requires Codex approval and zero-consumer confirmation.
proposed_action: Codex to verify zero consumers of payload_json across all DB views, functions, and reports before any change.
